reasoning: The baseline 6-month yield as of Feb 15, 2026, is 3.50% (the Friday Feb 13 observation). Over the past 10 days, the yield has slowly drifted from 3.54% down to exactly 3.50%, briefly ticking to 3.51%. Recent inflation data (CPI dropping to 2.4% YoY) and the expectation of upcoming rate cuts by the Fed (next FOMC meeting March 17-18) exert downward pressure on short-term treasury yields. For the first resolution date (Feb 22), the rate is more likely to remain flat at 3.50% or drop to 3.49% than strictly increase. However, daily fluctuations allow a reasonable chance (~42%) it random-walks to 3.51%+. For the later resolution date (March 17), the forecast horizon is wider. Following instructions to increase uncertainty for distant horizons, I push the probability closer to 0.50 (48%), acknowledging that strong upcoming GDP data or shifting Fed rhetoric ahead of the actual March meeting could easily cause a slight bounce in the 6-month yield, even if the primary trend remains mildly downward.
